'''Oseon 8920''' was an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality's]] [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]]. It was home to a criminal environment that made it resemble a miniature version of the infamous [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. The [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] former [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Director/Legends|director]] [[O'lif Khaz]] once maintained an [[Khaz's apartment|apartment]] on Oseon 8920. [[Sosten Freet]], the agent of the famous [[Musician/Legends|singer]] [[Ema Slake]], planted in the apartment one of Slake's trophies he had [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] and subsequently hired a group of individuals to investigate the theft. When investigators found the missing trophy at Khaz's apartment, Freet had Khaz arrested for burglary, thus deflecting attention from himself.

==Description==
Oseon 8920 was an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Oseon asteroid belt]], a part of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]].<ref name="Gamer" /> It was situated in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA Oseon" /> Along with the rest of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]], Oseon 8920 [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Oseon sun]]<ref name="LCFO" /> and was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Arleen system/Legends|Arleen system]], while the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route known as the [[Cadma Conduit]] linked it to the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]] and the<ref name="TEA Oseon" /> [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector's]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor]] and [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc systems]].<ref name="TEA Oseon" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
At some point, [[Sosten Freet]], the agent of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-renowned [[Musician/Legends|singer]] [[Ema Slake]], [[Thief/Legends|stole]] more than a half of the latter's [[Silicate/Legends|silicate]] trophies. In order to frame Slake's former [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Director/Legends|director]], the [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] [[O'lif Khaz]], for the crime and thus deflect attention from himself, Freet hired a group of individuals to investigate the theft. The process led the group to an [[Khaz's apartment|apartment]]—where Freet had planted one of the missing awards—that Khaz, wanting to evade a [[loan shark]] producer, maintained on Oseon 8920.<ref name="Gamer" />

There, the former director revealed to the investigators that Slake's retired manager, [[Phaldri Nex]], may have been privy to information regarding the theft of the trophies. Eventually, during the visit at Khaz's apartment, one of the investigators discovered the planted trophy. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], Freet appeared in the apartment in what was staged to be a coincidence and had the Kubaz arrested for burglary, although Khaz loudly proclaimed his innocence. Subsequently, Freet thanked the individuals, who were also financially compensated for their efforts. Upon leaving Oseon 8920, the group was captured by thugs affiliated with Nex, who, suspicious of Freet's involvement in the theft, later sent the group to break into the [[Freet's home|residence]] Slake's agent had on the asteroid [[Oseon 5631]].<ref name="Gamer" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Oseon 8920 was one of the several locations in the Oseon system that harbored criminal elements and thrived on its resultant odious reputation. Resembling a miniature version of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]<ref name="Gamer" />—a notorious [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>—Oseon 8920 sheltered such individuals as [[Gambler/Legends|gamblers]] hiding from loan sharks; loan sharks seeking to evade [[racketeering]] charges; and occasionally tourists wanting to economize on safer lodging.<ref name="Gamer" /> The [[Oseon Peacekeeper]]s,<ref name="LCFO" /> the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police force]] of the Oseon system, once intended to eliminate Oseon 8920's criminal environment but eventually relented as it proved too valuable as a source of information.<ref name="Gamer" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:A_Campaign_Guide_to_the_Centrality_G5.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Oseon 8920 was introduced in the article "A Campaign Guide to the Centrality."]]
Oseon 8920 was mentioned in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]," which was authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]<ref name="Gamer" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> In "A Campaign Guide to the Centrality," the asteroid serves as a setting for a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure hook, which this article assumes plays out as described.<ref name="Gamer" />

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Oseon system, and therefore Oseon 8920, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' placement by establishing that the Oseon system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA Oseon" />
